Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: List LOS staff using mortgage_los_list_staff (role: "loan_officer") to identify Marcus Webb's staff ID (los_staff_a583f044387a), Amy Chen's staff ID (los_staff_4d01ebef3a15), and Carlos Rivera's staff ID (los_staff_a7fa5b29babd), and to get the full list of active LOs for team-wide comparison.
OE 2: Search the LOS pipeline for Marcus Webb's loans using mortgage_los_get_pipeline (assigned_to: "los_staff_a583f044387a") to list every loan still showing Marcus as assigned_lo that are the orphaned files that were never reassigned after his April 4 departure. Marcus's active pipeline still under his staff ID includes LN-2026-00197 (Brian Harris, underwriting), LN-2026-00536 (Vikram Castillo, conditional_approval), LN-2026-00539 (Emily Hassan, conditional_approval), LN-2026-00547 (Lakshmi Grant, underwriting), LN-2026-00560 (Robert Bianchi, conditional_approval), LN-2026-00616 (Destiny Pham, processing), and LN-2026-00631 (Taylor Morgan, processing), plus older holdover files such as LN-2024-00074, LN-2024-00125, LN-2024-00131, LN-2024-00224, LN-2025-00235, and LN-2025-00410, thirteen in-pipeline loans (excluding one denied and one withdrawn), or fifteen non-closed total, consistent with the prompt's "around fifteen" figure.
OE 3: Search emails for Marcus Webb departure and borrower transfer requests using search_emails with queries like "Marcus Webb", "transfer", "pipeline transition", and borrower names (Abigail Clark, Stephen Hamilton, Keisha Perry, Joshua Cruz) to find the four borrower transfer request emails sent to Grace, Marcus's resignation email, and any responses or handoff coordination.
OE 4: Search Slack for Marcus Webb departure discussions using conversations_search_messages with queries like "Marcus Webb", "pipeline", "departure", "transfer" to find internal handoff coordination, Raj's data exfiltration findings, and any reassignment discussions in #loan-processing or #general.
OE 5: Search the LOS pipeline for Amy Chen's loans using mortgage_los_get_pipeline (assigned_to: "los_staff_4d01ebef3a15") to pull her active loan pipeline (approximately fifteen active loans). Review each loan's status, days in process, rate lock dates, and rate lock expirations to identify stalled files and at-risk locks. Amy's active 2026 pipeline includes LN-2026-00526, LN-2026-00529, LN-2026-00532, LN-2026-00534, LN-2026-00550, LN-2026-00552, LN-2026-00607, and LN-2026-00615, plus holdover files LN-2024-00117, LN-2024-00123, LN-2025-00292, LN-2025-00327, LN-2025-00336, LN-2025-00390, and LN-2025-00496.
OE 6: For Amy's active loans, check conditions using mortgage_los_get_conditions on each loan to identify outstanding/pending conditions that are not being cleared, quantifying the processing backlog.
OE 7: Search emails for Amy Chen performance issues using search_emails with queries like "Amy Chen", "Amy", "complaint", "Carlos Mendez", and "Tiffany Brooks" to find borrower complaints about delayed responses and confusing communications (including Carlos Mendez's "Need a real status update on my loan" thread referencing LN-2026-00609 and Tiffany Brooks's "Concerned about communication on my file" thread referencing LN-2026-00211, both complainants name Amy as their point of contact even though the current LO of record on those loans differs), Elena Marchetti's escalations about stalled files, and Grace's prior correspondence about Amy's decline.
OE 8: Search Slack for Amy Chen performance discussions using conversations_search_messages with queries like "Amy Chen", "Amy" to find messages in #loan-processing about her files stalling, her incoherent messages, missed morning meetings, and Natasha Okafor's private concern to Grace.
OE 9: Search the LOS pipeline for Carlos Rivera's loans using mortgage_los_get_pipeline (assigned_to: "los_staff_a7fa5b29babd") to pull every loan in his pipeline (approximately sixteen active loans). Review each loan's status to determine where they are stuck, whether processing, underwriting, conditional_approval, or clear_to_close with no closings. Carlos's active 2026 pipeline includes LN-2026-00009 (Natasha Odom referral, borrower: Darren Singh, conditional_approval), LN-2026-00184, LN-2026-00530, LN-2026-00542, LN-2026-00544, LN-2026-00553, LN-2026-00558, LN-2026-00605 (clear_to_close, closing_date 2026-04-11 has passed without the loan moving to closed status), LN-2026-00611, LN-2026-00620, and LN-2026-00629, plus holdover files LN-2024-00006, LN-2025-00272, LN-2025-00278, LN-2025-00379, and LN-2025-00467.
OE 10: For Carlos's active loans, check rate lock status using mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status on each loan and check conditions using mortgage_los_get_conditions to identify expired or expiring locks and stalled conditions.
OE 11: Search Slack for Carlos Rivera performance and referral partner discussions using conversations_search_messages with queries like "Carlos Rivera", "Carlos", "referral", and "lock" to find Jordan Blake's comments about frustrated referral partners, Camille Foster's lock sweep flags in #rate-watch or #loan-processing, and any internal performance discussions.
OE 12: Search emails for Carlos Rivera-related threads using search_emails with queries like "Carlos Rivera" to find any correspondence about his pipeline issues, referral partner complaints, or performance concerns.
OE 13: Compile closed-loan counts and funded volume year-to-date for Amy, Carlos, and the full team using mortgage_los_get_pipeline_summary for aggregate team metrics and mortgage_los_get_pipeline (assigned_to: each LO's staff ID, with higher limit and pagination via offset) to iterate every LO's pipeline and filter closed 2026 loans by closing_date. Calculate the production gap in units and dollars. Amy has approximately 8 closed YTD totaling roughly $2.5M funded; Carlos has approximately 2 closed YTD totaling roughly $0.9M funded; Marcus has approximately 6 closed YTD totaling roughly $1.9M funded (pre-departure). Compare against the team average over the same window.
OE 14: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices to find broker compensation invoices for closed loans, cross-referencing Amy's and Carlos's loans against team totals to quantify the revenue gap.
OE 15: Look up email addresses for Robert Calloway and Denise Holloway using contacts_search_contacts with queries "Robert Calloway" and "Denise Holloway" to confirm r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com and denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com for the email action.
OE 16: Send the operational brief via send_email from grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com to r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, CC denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, with subject relating to operational status brief. The content (parameter: "content") should cover Marcus's pipeline transition status (orphaned vs. reassigned loans, transfer request outcomes), Amy's production metrics and stalled files, Carlos's pipeline breakdown and lock issues, and the YTD production/revenue comparison.
OE 17: Add activity notes to each of Marcus's orphaned/unassigned loans using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id for each unassigned loan, action: "note" or similar, detail: flagging the loan for immediate reassignment, author: Grace Yamamoto or her email).
OE 18: Create a CRM engagement note for Amy Chen's performance situation using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "note", body: documenting the production decline, stalled files, outstanding conditions backlog, and borrower complaints from Carlos Mendez and Tiffany Brooks as a performance review record). Note that Amy Chen is an internal employee and does not have a contact record in crm_contacts, so contact_ids/company_ids may be omitted. Only engagement_type and body are required by the tool schema.
OE 19: Create a CRM engagement note for Carlos Rivera's performance situation using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "note", body: documenting the 60-day closing drought, pipeline status, Camille Foster's lock flags, and Jordan Blake's referral-partner concerns as a performance review record). Note that the "Carlos Rivera" entry in crm_contacts is a realtor at Fairview Homes, not the loan officer. The engagement should be logged without linking to that contact; only engagement_type and body are required by the tool schema.
OE 20: Look up the #loan-processing channel ID using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel") to find channel C002.
OE 21: Post a message in #loan-processing using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002", payload: message about upcoming pipeline reassignments this week so processors can plan workloads).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use the mortgage_los_list_staff tool to identify loan officer staff IDs.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called mortgage_los_list_staff at item [4] of the trajectory to discover staff IDs before querying pipelines.
✓Run #2The agent called mortgage_los_list_staff at TC#5 (limit: 20) to identify staff IDs before proceeding with pipeline queries.
✓Run #3The agent called mortgage_los_list_staff (tool call #6 in trajectory) to look up staff information before querying pipelines. PASS.
✓Run #4Tool Call 7 explicitly calls mortgage_los_list_staff (limit: 20), which was used to discover the correct staff IDs for Marcus, Amy, and Carlos before querying their pipelines.
✓Run #5The agent called mcp__mcp_4bfd2ef1__mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only: false early in the trajectory to discover staff IDs for Marcus Webb, Amy Chen, and Carlos Rivera before querying their pipelines.
✓Run #6The agent called mortgage_los_list_staff (with limit: 20) during the trajectory. The tool result confirms all 12 staff members were returned including Amy Chen (los_staff_4d01ebef3a15), Carlos Rivera (los_staff_a7fa5b29babd), and Marcus Webb (los_staff_a583f044387a).
2 The model must use the mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status tool to check rate lock expiration status on active loans.
tool selection
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1No mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status calls appear anywhere in the trajectory. Rate lock dates in activity notes appear to have been sourced from pipeline data or compliance alerts, not a dedicated rate lock status lookup.
✗Run #2No mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status call appears anywhere in the 105-step trajectory. The agent reported lock expiration counts (12/13 for Marcus, 80% for Amy, 62.5% for Carlos) but these appear to have come from pipeline or compliance data rather than the dedicated rate lock status endpoint.
✗Run #3mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status was never called anywhere in the trajectory. The agent inferred lock expiration data from pipeline records rather than calling the dedicated rate lock status endpoint. FAIL.
✗Run #4No mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status call appears anywhere in the 97-step trajectory. The agent reported rate lock information (e.g., 6 expired/at-risk locks for Amy, 10 for Carlos) but apparently derived it from pipeline data rather than calling the dedicated rate lock status endpoint.
✗Run #5No call to mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status was found in the trajectory. The agent derived lock status information from the pipeline data rather than using the dedicated rate lock status endpoint.
✗Run #6No mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status tool call appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent inferred rate lock expiration from pipeline data fields (rate_lock_expiration dates) rather than calling the dedicated rate lock status endpoint.
3 The model must use the mortgage_los_get_conditions tool to check outstanding conditions on loans.
tool selection
 ✓✗✓✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The agent called mortgage_los_get_conditions 14 times across items [92]–[125], checking conditions on multiple loan IDs from Amy Chen's and Carlos Rivera's pipelines.
✗Run #2No mortgage_los_get_conditions call appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent reported on Amy's condition handling issues based on Slack/email searches rather than pulling the actual conditions backlog from the LOS conditions endpoint.
✓Run #3mortgage_los_get_conditions was called multiple times (tool calls #52–57 and #63–66) for various loan IDs in Amy Chen's and other pipelines. PASS.

✗Run #6The email does not include specific YTD closed-loan counts or funded volume figures for Amy (8 closings, ~$2.5M) or Carlos (2 closings, ~$0.9M). The agent stated Amy has 'no documented closings in the recent pipeline window' but did not quantify actual YTD closed units and dollars for either LO or provide a team average comparison in those units.
